page: Amazon Web Services g5.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 192 vCPUs (96 physical cores) running at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, the instance bundles eight NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with a combined 179 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core performance is rated as poor in stress-ng tests and average in Geekbench, multi-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng and the top 10% for Passmark CPU tests, including physics and encryption. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads ranking in the top 10% and large-block reads/writes in the top 25%. The instance excels in parallel processing and graphics tasks, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench ray tracing and LLM inference benchmarks across small, medium, and large models.

The primary tradeoff of the g5.48xlarge is its lower single-threaded performance, making it less suitable for sequential, single-core bound applications. However, its massive resource density—combining eight GPUs, high-speed local NVMe storage, and high memory bandwidth—delivers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized enterprise workloads. This hardware profile is optimized for large-scale LLM inference, machine learning training, ray tracing, high-throughput database operations, and intensive graphics rendering.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.48xlarge is a high-density, GPU-accelerated instance designed for parallel computing and graphics-intensive workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 192 vCPUs, 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and eight NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with 179 GB of total VRAM. Storage is supported by 7600 GB of local NVMe SSDs, complemented by 100 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. While single-core benchmarks are relatively modest, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic performance. It is highly optimized for large-scale LLM inference, ray tracing, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR4 RAM, eight NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs, and 7600 GB of NVMe SSD storage for demanding parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 192 vCPUs, eight NVIDIA A10G GPUs, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ultra-high-density parallel computing platform featuring eight hardware accelerators, massive local NVMe storage, and high-bandwidth networking for enterprise AI workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with 179 GB total VRAM for parallel acceleration
192 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 768 GB DDR4 memory for massive multi-threaded workloads
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and ray tracing benchmarks
Optimized for machine learning, database operations, and graphics rendering
